a260712p1.summary: Justice commissioner Michael McGrath says Brussels seeks to strengthen social media safeguards — Brussels is preparing …
a260712p1.title: EU Justice Commissioner Michael McGrath says the EC is set to propose new digital rules by year-end aimed at protecting consumers from online spending traps (Laura Dubois/Financial Times)

a260712p10.summary: The founder of Chinese AI lab Zhipu argued that frontier artificial intelligence should remain broadly accessible rather …
a260712p10.title: Memo: Tang Jie, founder of Z.ai, the Chinese lab behind the GLM models, argues frontier AI capabilities should stay "as open and widely accessible as possible" (Bloomberg)

a260712p11.summary: Elevated to fix Microsoft's struggling gaming division, she is making tough decisions about its future
a260712p11.title: A profile of Asha Sharma, an outsider elevated by Satya Nadella to lead Xbox, where she made sweeping job cuts and shed game studios within her first few months (Financial Times)

a260712p12.summary: Touted as a way to engage visitors and boost funding, new tools are triggering concerns around trust and ethics
a260712p12.title: How museums are using AI chatbots to reach new audiences, as some museum staff worry AI-generated inaccuracies and bias could undermine them as trusted sources (Naomi Rea/Financial Times)

a260712p2.summary: Vendelux, a company that helps CMOs figure out their live events strategy, raised $50 million. — It plans to use the new round …
a260712p2.title: NYC-based Vendelux, a live B2B event information platform for CMOs and marketing and sales teams, raised a $50M Series B led by Tribeca Venture Partners (Lucia Moses/Business Insider)

a260712p3.summary: Hotel bookings fall short of expectations as price-conscious fans opt to stay in American homes
a260712p3.title: AirDNA: during the FIFA World Cup group stage, platforms such as Airbnb saw 52K+ new listings in US host cities, while hotel bookings fell short of expectations (Financial Times)

a260712p4.summary: The resistance to artificial intelligence is growing over fears about human extinction—but one activist's disappearance has the movement on edge
a260712p4.title: A look at the growing anti-AI movement in the Bay Area, as the disappearance of Sam Kirchner, co-founder of a hard-line activist group, has the movement on edge (Zusha Elinson/Wall Street Journal)

a260712p5.summary: Our obsession with AI code-writing tools is overwhelming the web's unsung human caretakers. If you are reading the digital version …
a260712p5.title: Users of AI coding tools are flooding open-source projects with low-quality contributions, overwhelming maintainers and potentially eroding community engagement (Sam Learner/Financial Times)

a260712p6.summary: Also: New Apple Pencils are coming. — Apple's new Mac chip road map represents the company's latest move to rebuild …
a260712p6.title: Sources: Apple is already taping out M7 with major NPU upgrades, and plans M7 Ultra with 1.5TB RAM, and M8, for 2028; new Pencils are coming with a new iPad Pro (Mark Gurman/Bloomberg)

a260712p7.summary: In a post on X, the company said, “We're extending Claude Fable 5 access on all paid plans, as well as keeping Claude Code's weekly rate limits 50% higher, through July 19.”
a260712p7.title: Anthropic says it is extending Claude Fable 5 access on all paid plans, as well as keeping Claude Code's weekly rate limits 50% higher, through July 19 (The Economic Times)

a260712p8.summary: Three prominent artificial intelligence developers released new models over the past week. They all promise to be more advanced …
a260712p8.title: OpenAI, Meta, and SpaceXAI may be able to put pressure on Anthropic by emphasizing cost efficiency, as business customers increasingly scrutinize AI spending (Bloomberg)

a260712p9.summary: Cursor is developing a general-purpose AI agent meant to compete with popular tools like Anthropic's Claude Cowork …
a260712p9.title: Sources: Cursor is building a general-purpose AI agent codenamed Sand, aimed at non-developers, that handles emails, texts, and documents to rival Claude Cowork (Grace Kay/The Information)
